name: ExtensionMolecularSequence_Relative_CoordinateSystem
title: R5: Ways of identifying nucleotides or amino acids within a sequence (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` 1..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` 1..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` is part of an existing definition because parent element `MolecularSequence.relative` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` has a context of MolecularSequence based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MolecularSequence`.
Element `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Ways of identifying nucleotides or amino acids within a sequence (new)
element.comment:
Element `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` is part of an existing definition because parent element `MolecularSequence.relative` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` has a context of MolecularSequence based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MolecularSequence`.
Element `MolecularSequence.relative.coordinateSystem` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Ways of identifying nucleotides or amino acids within a sequence
element.definition: These are different ways of identifying nucleotides or amino acids within a sequence. Different databases and file types may use different systems. For detail definitions, see https://loinc.org/92822-6/ for more detail.
